breakfast.name: Veggie & Egg Scramble
breakfast.description: 3 eggs scrambled with bell pepper, spinach, onion, and a sprinkle of feta. Served with sliced avocado and coffee.

lunch.name: Grilled Chicken & Quinoa Bowl
lunch.description: Sliced grilled chicken breast over quinoa with cherry tomatoes, cucumber, red onion, and a lemon-herb vinaigrette.

dinner.name: New Orleans Red Beans & Rice
dinner.description: Classic Monday-style red beans (kidney beans) simmered with andouille sausage, the holy trinity (onion, celery, green pepper), garlic, bay leaf, and Cajun seasoning. Served over brown rice with a side of crusty French bread. Sunday's a good day to cook this low-and-slow.

snack.name: Apple & Almond Butter
snack.description: One large apple sliced, served with 2 tbsp natural almond butter.

breakfast.name: Greek Yogurt Parfait
breakfast.description: Full-fat Greek yogurt layered with mixed berries, a drizzle of honey, and a handful of granola. Quick, high-protein, no cooking.

lunch.name: Red Beans & Rice Leftovers
lunch.description: Monday is red beans day in New Orleans — leftovers from Sunday get even better overnight. Reheat and eat.

dinner.name: Blackened Tilapia Tacos
dinner.description: Tilapia fillets rubbed with blackening spice and seared in a cast iron skillet. Served in corn tortillas with shredded purple cabbage, sliced avocado, and a squeeze of lime. Quick and satisfying on a Monday night.

snack.name: Hard-Boiled Eggs & Hot Sauce
snack.description: 2 hard-boiled eggs with a few dashes of Crystal or Tabasco. Very New Orleans. Very easy.

breakfast.name: Peanut Butter Banana Protein Smoothie
breakfast.description: Blend: 1 banana, 1 scoop vanilla protein powder, 1 tbsp peanut butter, 1 cup almond milk, handful of spinach (invisible, trust it), and ice. 400+ calories, done in 3 minutes.

lunch.name: Turkey & Avocado Wrap
lunch.description: Whole wheat tortilla with sliced turkey breast, avocado, romaine, tomato, and a swipe of Dijon mustard. Eat at your desk without guilt.

dinner.name: Sheet Pan Shrimp & Vegetables
dinner.description: Large Gulf shrimp tossed with olive oil, Cajun seasoning, garlic, and lemon zest. Spread on a sheet pan with zucchini, bell peppers, and cherry tomatoes. Roast at 400°F for 15 minutes. One pan, minimal cleanup.

snack.name: Mixed Nuts & Dark Chocolate
snack.description: Small handful of raw mixed nuts and 1-2 squares of 70%+ dark chocolate. Good fats, antioxidants, not sad.

breakfast.name: Avocado Toast with Eggs
breakfast.description: Two slices of whole grain toast topped with smashed avocado, a pinch of red pepper flakes, and two over-easy eggs on top. Simple, filling, solid.

lunch.name: Tuna Salad Lettuce Wraps
lunch.description: Canned albacore tuna mixed with a little olive oil mayo, Dijon, diced celery, and lemon juice. Spooned into large romaine or butter lettuce leaves. High protein, zero cooking.

dinner.name: Chicken & Black Bean Burrito Bowl
dinner.description: Seasoned ground chicken or sliced chicken thigh over brown rice with black beans, corn, salsa, Greek yogurt (instead of sour cream), and a handful of shredded Mexican cheese. Fast, filling, better than takeout.

snack.name: Cottage Cheese & Pineapple
snack.description: 1 cup cottage cheese with a scoop of fresh or canned pineapple chunks. High protein, underrated combo.

breakfast.name: Overnight Oats
breakfast.description: Prep the night before: rolled oats, almond milk, chia seeds, a spoonful of peanut butter, and sliced banana in a jar. Refrigerate overnight. Grab and go Thursday morning.

lunch.name: Spinach Salad with Grilled Chicken
lunch.description: Baby spinach, pre-cooked grilled chicken, sliced strawberries, toasted pecans, and a light balsamic vinaigrette. Use rotisserie chicken if you're short on time.

dinner.name: Gulf Shrimp Étouffée (Lightened Up)
dinner.description: A nod to the classics: Gulf shrimp sautéed in a butter-based roux with the trinity, garlic, and Cajun seasoning. Lighter than the full restaurant version but still deeply satisfying. Served over brown rice. This one's worth 30 minutes.

snack.name: Hummus & Veggies
snack.description: Store-bought hummus with sliced cucumber, carrots, and bell pepper strips. Keep it in the fridge for grab-and-snack moments.

breakfast.name: Smoked Salmon Scrambled Eggs
breakfast.description: 3 eggs softly scrambled with cream cheese and folded with smoked salmon. A little indulgent for Friday morning, ready in 8 minutes.

lunch.name: Rotisserie Chicken & Sweet Potato
lunch.description: Pick up a rotisserie chicken. Microwave a medium sweet potato (5 min). Pull chicken over the potato with a drizzle of olive oil and salt. Effortless Friday lunch.

dinner.name: Cast Iron NY Strip with Roasted Asparagus
dinner.description: A well-seasoned 10-12oz NY strip seared in a cast iron skillet with butter and garlic, finished in the oven. Served with roasted asparagus and a simple side salad. You've earned a real meal on Friday night.

snack.name: Protein Bar
snack.description: Keep a quality bar (RXBAR, Barebells, or Built) on hand for Friday afternoon when willpower is low and a meeting ran long.

breakfast.name: Cajun Breakfast Skillet
breakfast.description: Saturday morning cook-up: diced Andouille or smoked sausage, potatoes (or frozen hash browns), onion, bell pepper, and eggs cracked right into the skillet. Season with Tony Chachere's. One pan, full flavor, very Louisiana.

lunch.name: Shrimp Po'Boy (Lightened)
lunch.description: Gulf shrimp lightly seasoned and sautéed (not deep-fried), served on a toasted French bread roll with shredded lettuce, tomato, pickles, and a remoulade made from Greek yogurt + hot sauce + lemon. Dressed, the New Orleans way.

dinner.name: Grilled Salmon with Mango Salsa
dinner.description: Salmon fillet marinated in olive oil, garlic, and lime. Grilled or pan-seared. Topped with a quick mango-red onion-cilantro salsa. Served with steamed broccoli and brown rice. Clean eating that doesn't feel like punishment.

snack.name: Frozen Banana Bites
snack.description: Slice a banana, dip pieces in dark chocolate, freeze for 30 minutes. Prep while lunch is cooking. Saturday-level effort for a treat that actually feels like one.

produce: Bell peppers (red, green, yellow — get a bag)
Fresh mango (1) or frozen mango chunks
protein: Gulf shrimp, large (2 lbs, fresh or frozen)
pantry: Cajun seasoning (Tony Chachere's — this is non-negotiable in New Orleans)
French bread loaf (1, for po'boy + red beans)
other: Balsamic vinaigrette (store-bought or make your own)
Hot sauce: Crystal AND Tabasco (because yes, both)
Frozen hash browns (optional shortcut for Saturday skillet)

notes: This plan is built for a single person — portions are single-serve unless noted. Sunday's red beans intentionally make leftovers for Monday lunch (that's the New Orleans way — Monday is red beans day). Wednesday evening, spend 5 minutes prepping Thursday's overnight oats before bed. Rotisserie chicken on Friday is your cheat code when the week catches up with you. The shrimp étouffée on Thursday and the po'boy on Saturday are your two 'I live in New Orleans' meals — don't skip them. Swap any fish day with shrimp or chicken if fresh seafood looks rough at the store. All proteins can be batch-cooked if you have 30 minutes on Sunday, but the plan works fine cooking fresh each day. Total estimated grocery run: $120–$160 at any major New Orleans grocery (Rouses, Whole Foods, or Winn-Dixie).
